description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped stone with a roughly cylindrical form, tapering slightly at one end and featuring a flat, broad face on one side. The surface is coarse and uneven, with visible pitting, chips, and natural textural variations typical of quarried or weathered stone. The coloration is predominantly a muted, dusty beige with subtle hints of orange-brown on the uppermost surface, suggesting exposure to the elements or mineral deposits. One side of the stone displays a pronounced vertical groove or indentation, possibly from natural erosion or mechanical impact. The overall mass and bulk of the stone indicate it could serve as a substantial physical barrier in an urban setting, such as a boundary marker or traffic deterrent. There are no visible artificial carvings, inscriptions, or signs of recent modification, giving it a raw, utilitarian appearance.
description: A large, rough beige stone with an irregular, cylindrical shape and a prominent groove on one side. Its coarse, weathered surface and substantial mass make it suitable as a physical barrier in urban environments.
description_view_0: Tall, narrow profile with a rounded top and visible surface roughness; the left side appears slightly indented.
description_view_1: Broad, flat face with minimal curvature and a generally uniform texture; the top edge is slightly chipped.
description_view_2: Narrowest side, showing the elongated cylindrical form and pronounced surface irregularities.
description_view_3: Wide face with a deep vertical groove or indentation on the right, and a rough, angular edge on the left.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or boundary marker, upright on the ground
attribute_pattern: No regular pattern; surface is mottled and pitted with natural textural variations and a single deep groove.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered, with visible pitting, minor chipping, and some discoloration from exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or built environments but may blend with other natural stone features or landscaping elements.
